title: rubus × ghilcosensis
intro: Rubus × ghilcosensis, also known as the hybrid bramble or raspberry, is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family, resulting from a cross between Rubus idaeus and Rubus fruticosus. The leaves and roots of Rubus × ghilcosensis have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Rubus × ghilcosensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
